Object.Name: PoE Management
Object.Description1: This LwM2M Object provides monitoring and control of Power over Ethernet (PoE) functionality on devices that support IEEE 802.3af/at/bt standards. It enables remote monitoring of PoE power input including voltage, current, and power consumption. For Powered Devices (PD), it provides visibility into the power being received. The object supports detection of PoE standard in use (af, at, bt), power class negotiation, and power cycling for recovery scenarios.

Item.Name: PoE Supported
Item.Description: Indicates whether the device hardware supports Power over Ethernet. True if PoE circuitry is present and functional, false if the device does not have PoE capability.

Item.Name: PoE Enabled
Item.Description: Enable or disable PoE power input. When disabled, the device will not draw power from the PoE source even if available. Useful for power management or when using alternative power sources.

Item.Name: Input Voltage
Item.Description: Current PoE input voltage in volts. Typical values: 48V for 802.3af/at, 52-57V for 802.3bt. Returns 0 if PoE is not active or not connected.

Item.Name: Input Current
Item.Description: Current being drawn from the PoE source in amperes. Maximum depends on PoE class: ~350mA for Class 1, ~600mA for Class 3, ~950mA for Class 4, up to 1900mA for 802.3bt Type 4.

Item.Name: Input Power
Item.Description: Current power consumption from PoE source in watts. Calculated as Input Voltage x Input Current. Maximum values: 15.4W (af), 30W (at), 60W (bt Type 3), 90W+ (bt Type 4).

Item.Name: PoE Standard
Item.Description: Detected or configured PoE standard: "802.3af" (PoE, 15.4W), "802.3at" (PoE+, 30W), "802.3bt" (PoE++, 60-90W+), or "None" if PoE is not active.

Item.Name: PoE Class
Item.Description: Negotiated PoE power class (0-8). Class 0-3: 802.3af (0=default 15.4W, 1=4W, 2=7W, 3=15.4W). Class 4: 802.3at (30W). Class 5-8: 802.3bt (5=45W, 6=60W, 7=75W, 8=90W+).

Item.Name: Maximum Power
Item.Description: Maximum power available from the PSE (Power Sourcing Equipment) based on negotiated class and PSE capability. This is the upper limit the device can draw.

Item.Name: Power Allocated
Item.Description: Power allocated by the PSE for this device. May differ from Maximum Power based on PSE power budget management and LLDP-MED negotiation.

Item.Name: Temperature
Item.Description: PoE controller or PD interface chip temperature in degrees Celsius. High temperatures may indicate overload or thermal issues. Operating range typically -40 to 85C.

Item.Name: Status
Item.Description: Human-readable status string indicating PoE state: "Active" (powered via PoE), "Inactive" (PoE not in use), "Detecting" (PoE detection in progress), "Fault" (PoE error), "Disabled" (PoE disabled).

Item.Name: Reset PoE
Item.Description: Execute to perform a PoE power cycle. Temporarily disables PoE input then re-enables it, forcing re-negotiation with the PSE. Useful for recovering from fault conditions.
